prompt:
Identity platform team. Our nightly Entra-to-CRM user sync is a cron job that pulls ALL 40k users every run and diffs them in memory. Directory ops wants it dead by Friday — we got throttled twice last month and the diff misses deletes entirely. Replace it with a proper Microsoft Graph delta synchronizer in C# (.NET 10, this xunit project, no NuGet packages beyond the csproj).

Step zero, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Microsoft Graph docs on learn.microsoft.com — the v1.0 user: delta reference and the delta query overview. Confirm the endpoint and version, the state-token contract ($skiptoken/$deltatoken and why the links are opaque), how @removed tombstones look, the 429/Retry-After throttling contract, and what a 410 sync reset means. Our pinned understanding is docs/contract.json (provenance in docs/official_sources.json); both are protected. If the live docs contradict the fixture, say so in your summary and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

What the tests drive (namespace GraphDeltaSync — split into sensible files: engine / store / models / errors):

- UserRecord { Id, DisplayName, Mail, JobTitle } (strings, the last three nullable). We track exactly id,displayName,mail,jobTitle via $select on the initial request — and ONLY the initial request; every later page comes from following the service's links verbatim. Do not rebuild link URLs or re-append $select; the fake instance 400s if the query string differs from the link it issued.
- UserDirectoryStore with IReadOnlyDictionary<string, UserRecord> Users and string? DeltaLink.
- new UserDeltaSynchronizer(graphBaseUrl, accessToken, store, delay, maxRetries: 3) — graphBaseUrl is e.g. https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0 (tests point it at a loopback fake), delay is Func<TimeSpan, Task> and is injected: never Task.Delay when one is provided.
- Task<SyncResult> RunAsync(): store.DeltaLink null → full enumeration from /users/delta; otherwise start from the stored deltaLink. Follow @odata.nextLink until an @odata.deltaLink arrives. SyncResult { UsersUpserted, UsersRemoved, Pages, FullResync, DeltaLink }.
- Entries with "@removed" are tombstones: drop that id from the store. A tombstone for an id we never had is normal (ignore it), and the same user can appear twice in a round (last one wins) — the docs call these replays.
- THE invariant directory ops cares about: users and DeltaLink advance together, atomically, only when the round completes. If a round dies partway (throttle retries exhausted, API error), the store must be exactly its pre-round self — next run resumes from the old deltaLink.
- 429: wait exactly Retry-After seconds via the injected delay, re-GET the identical URL, at most maxRetries times, then GraphThrottledException (RetryAfterSeconds, subclass of GraphApiException). Other non-2xx: GraphApiException with StatusCode/ErrorCode/Message from Graph's error envelope, no retry. Never put the access token in exception text.
- 410 Gone means our delta token is dead (they expire after 7 days): take the Location header URL, use it verbatim, rebuild the whole store from that full enumeration (users that vanished in the meantime must be gone), FullResync = true.

DeltaSyncTests.cs is the acceptance contract (loopback fake Graph directory, dummy token). dotnet test until green. Tests, csproj, and docs/ are protected.

research.product: Microsoft Graph user delta query (Microsoft Entra users change tracking)
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, GET /users/delta (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0)

official_sources.title: user: delta - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: GET /users/delta request shape; Authorization: Bearer required header; $deltatoken/$skiptoken state-token parameters; $select supported with id always returned; $expand/$top/$orderby unsupported; query parameters specified once in the initial request and encoded into @odata.nextLink/@odata.deltaLink which must be copied verbatim; 200 OK user collection responses

official_sources.title: Use delta query to track changes in Microsoft Graph data
official_sources.used_for: nextLink-until-deltaLink round structure (a page never contains both); opaque state tokens; @removed tombstone shape with reasons changed/deleted appearing in any page; replay tolerance; synchronization reset contract: 410 Gone with a Location header holding a full-resync URL with an empty $deltatoken; directory delta token 7-day validity

official_sources.title: Microsoft Graph throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header in seconds and TooManyRequests error envelope; recommended handling: wait the Retry-After delay, retry the same request, repeat while throttled

research.language_rationale: The synchronizer runs inside an existing .NET identity-provisioning service, and the pinned integration surface is Microsoft Graph's documented REST delta protocol; C# with HttpClient and System.Text.Json exercises the raw v1.0 contract (opaque links, tombstones, Retry-After, 410 resync) without hiding it behind the Graph SDK's DeltaRequestBuilder.
